description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a dual-wing configuration, featuring a predominantly olive green fuselage and maroon wings adorned with white roundel insignias. The aircraft has an open cockpit situated near the front, with a visible pilot figure and a mounted machine gun. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a tail skid, and the undercarriage includes struts and braces typical of early 20th-century aircraft. The tail section is conventional, with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The wings are straight and braced with interplane struts and wires, and the propeller is mounted at the nose. The overall design suggests a World War I-era scout or reconnaissance airplane, likely constructed from a combination of wood, fabric, and metal.
description: A vintage biplane with maroon wings, olive green fuselage, open cockpit, and classic dual-wing structure, featuring a nose-mounted propeller and visible landing gear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual wings, open cockpit, nose-mounted propeller, and main landing gear.
description_view_1: Angled top-rear view highlighting the maroon wings with insignias, tail section, and cockpit placement.
description_view_2: Top-down view displaying the full wingspan, cockpit, and symmetrical layout of the wings and tail.
description_view_3: Angled top-rear view from the opposite side, emphasizing the tail, wing structure, and engine cowling.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and supported by interplane struts and wires; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit located just behind the engine cowling.
